title: Mepazine Hydrochloride as a New Reagent for the Spectrophotometric Determination of Ruthenium(lll)
author: H. SANKE GOWDA
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Post-graduate Studies and Research in Chemistry, University  

 of Mysore, Manasa Gangotri, Mysore 570006, India. 

  Manuscript received 12 July 1976, accepted 10 February 1977.  

 Mepazine hydrochloride forms a red coloured complex with ruthenium(III) at room temperature in acid medium. The red complex exhibits absorption maxi­mum at 512-516 am. Beer's law is valid over the concentration ranges 0.40-12.8 ppm in sulphuric acid and 0.32-6.4 ppm in hydrochloric acid. The Sandell's sensi­tivity of the reaction is 0,022  \(\mu\) g/cm 2 . in H 2 SO 4  and 0.015  µg  cm 2  in HCI. The respective molar absorptivity is 4.48 x 10 3  1 mol -1 cm -1  in H 2 SO 4  and 6.7 x 10 3  1 mol -1  cm -1  in HCI. The composition study by molar ratio method indicates that the ruthenium-MH complex has a ratio of 1 : 2.
